message:
feat: expand core FS layer, Btrfs backend, and MVCC subsystem

Broad expansion across the filesystem stack with ~1770 insertions
touching the block layer, Btrfs implementation, core abstractions,
inode management, and MVCC concurrency control:

- ffs-block: extend block allocation and layout primitives for larger
  extent trees and improved fragmentation resistance
- ffs-btrfs: expand B-tree operations and copy-on-write semantics to
  support additional Btrfs-compatible on-disk structures
- ffs-core: augment core filesystem trait surface with new metadata
  operations and error variants
- ffs-inode: refine inode lifecycle management and attribute handling
- ffs-mvcc: enhance multi-version concurrency control with improved
  snapshot isolation and conflict detection
- ffs-mvcc/tests: expand MVCC stress test suite to cover new concurrent
  access patterns and edge cases
- Update beads issue tracker with current task status

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
